expectations.evidence: Issue #1 (High): 'No semantic sectioning elements used. The page has no <header>, <nav>, <main>, or <footer> landmarks. All content is placed directly inside <body>.' Also issues #2, #3, #6 all reference missing landmarks.

expectations.text: Detected missing alt attribute on logo img
expectations.evidence: Issue #4 (Critical, line 30): 'Image is missing the alt attribute entirely. Screen readers will fall back to reading the file name (logo.png), which is not meaningful to users.'
Issue #13 (Pass, line 2): 'lang="en" attribute is correctly set, identifying the page language for screen readers and search engines.'

expectations.text: Report includes a dedicated semantic HTML review section
expectations.evidence: Report has a dedicated 'Semantic HTML Issues' section (h2) with a table of 4 semantic HTML issues separate from the 'Accessibility Issues' section.
Report uses Critical/High/Medium/Low/Pass severity badges instead of the standard ✅/❌/⚠️/➖/🔵 classification system.
